_comment: The COSMETIC CATALOG — every appearance piece the player could wear, defined as data (no code changes to add one). This is the 'inventory' half of player customization: which items exist, what SLOT each fills, and which WORLD granted it. The player's owned set + equipped loadout live separately (server-canonical PlayerAppearance); this file is the shared dictionary both the wardrobe UI and the renderer look items up in. Presentation-only: nothing here touches game logic. A 'sheet' that doesn't exist degrades silently (the layer is skipped), matching SpriteSlot's discipline, so new items can be declared before their art is drawn. Art is generated by tools/gen_cosmetics.py — remember to run 'godot --headless --path pokepals --import' after regenerating so new PNGs get imported.
_slots_comment: SLOTS are the paper-doll layers, drawn back-to-front by ascending 'z'. Exactly one item occupies a slot at a time. 'required':true means the slot must always resolve to something — PlayerAppearance falls back to the slot's base default so the avatar can never render empty. Leave room between z values so new layers can slot between without renumbering. 'body' is the bare (skin-dyed) figure; 'accessory' (glasses/scarf) sits over the outfit but under hair.
_color_slots_comment: COLOR slots are the 'palette' half of paper-doll + palette: cheap personalization that recolors a GRAYSCALE layer without new art (skin tone, hair color). 'applies_to' names the paper-doll slot the recolor targets; 'ramps' are the named choices; 'default' is what a fresh player gets; 'swatches' gives each ramp a base [r,g,b] (0..1) that AvatarCompositor maps the layer's luminance onto (dark outline preserved, mid/light -> shadow/highlight of the swatch). Adding a color is a data edit — no new art.
_items_comment: ITEMS keyed by a WORLD-NAMESPACED id ('origin:name'), mirroring the companion's area ids ('world:region') — so a world's drops are self-contained and a later authoritative server can grant them by id without collision. 'origin':'base' items are owned by every player from the start; exactly one per default-able slot is tagged 'default_for' (the fresh-player loadout: an average build, a tunic, boots, short hair + the default skin/hair colors — a clothed starter). The render fields (sheet/frame/fps/walk_frames/idle_frame/dirs/z) follow the SAME sheet convention as the existing player/companion art (rows=facing down/side/up, cols=walk cycle, left=side flipped); see SpriteActor. Body + hair sheets are GRAYSCALE (recolored by their color slot); outfit/footwear/headwear/accessory carry baked colors.
